prompt.task: Prepare a Product Requirements Document (PRD) for processing by a 5-agent team (Product Owner, Scrum Master, 2 Builders, 1 Reviewer) by analyzing structure, identifying cross-cutting concerns, and dividing into logical chunks for parallel processing.

context_loading.instructions: Before proceeding, read the context documentation inventory: - Read: .claude/docs/.context-docs-inventory.xml Based on the PRD's technical requirements, read relevant context: - For system patterns: Read architecture/service-patterns.md - For state management: Read architecture/state-management.md - For performance considerations: Read architecture/performance-optimization.md - For debugging approach: Read debugging/debugging-system-overview.md

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.usage: For accessing documentation on APIs/libraries and verifying technical details

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: For CRUD operations to store outputs and reference codebase files

tool.name: sequential_thinking_mcp
tool.usage: For analyzing PRD structure, identifying cross-cutting functionality, and dividing logically

step.title: Parse New PRD Structure
step.description: Analyze the structured PRD content including executive summary, objectives, target users, feature specifications, technical requirements, and scope. Extract core features from feature_specification section and technical impact from technical_requirements section.

step.title: Identify Cross-Cutting Concerns
step.description: From the technical_requirements section, identify shared components, database schemas, APIs, and file dependencies that span multiple features. Use the file_impact analysis to understand code boundaries.

step.title: Create Logical Chunks
step.description: Divide PRD into 2-3 logical implementation chunks based on: - Core features from feature_specification - Technical boundaries from file_impact analysis - Acceptance criteria complexity - Parallel development feasibility Each chunk should be 3-5k tokens and implementable by separate teams.

step.title: Extract Chunk Context
step.description: For each chunk, extract relevant context from PRD sections: - User personas and use cases that apply to this chunk - Specific acceptance criteria from the feature_specification - Technical requirements and file paths from technical_requirements - Scope items and constraints that affect this chunk - Associated risks from risks_and_mitigation

step.title: Define Dependencies
step.description: Map dependencies between chunks using the technical_requirements and scope_and_constraints sections. Identify which chunks can be developed in parallel vs. sequentially.

step.title: Create Chunk Summaries
step.description: Generate 50-100 word summaries for each chunk including purpose, technical scope, user value, and dependencies. Reference the primary persona and use cases affected.

step.title: Update GitHub Projects
step.description: For each chunk: - Create chunk issue using Feature Chunk template: gh issue create --repo MLorneSmith/2025slideheroes --template feature-chunk.yml - Fill template fields: - Chunk Title: "[CHUNK-{#}] {EPIC_NAME} - {CHUNK_FOCUS}" - Parent Epic: "#{EPIC_NUMBER}" - Chunk Summary: 50-100 word summary from chunk analysis - Technical Scope: Components and file changes from chunk content - Priority: Inherit from Epic (will be refined in Priority Matrix during validation) - Technical Domain Labels: Based on file_impact analysis - Cross-cutting Dependencies: From dependency analysis - Apply labels: ["chunk", "needs-validation"] plus domain labels - Move Epic to "Chunks" column in Planning Board - Store chunks in: .claude/build/3-output/{epic-name}/2-chunks/ - Create context files: .claude/contexts/chunk-{id}/context.md

input.prd_content: <prd><title>PRD: tRPC Migration for Prompts and Integrations</title><features><feature name="Prompts Router"><description>CRUD operations, likes, and search for prompts.</description><database_tables>prompts, prompt_likes</database_tables></feature><feature name="Integrations Router"><description>API key management for AI providers.</description><database_tables>user_api_keys</database_tables></feature></features><technical_requirements><libraries>tRPC, Zod, Drizzle-ORM, Supabase</libraries></technical_requirements></prd>

chunk.summary: This chunk covers the Prompts tRPC router for `/prompts` page, enabling CRUD operations, likes, and search with tRPC, Zod, and Drizzle-ORM. It targets `prompts` and `prompt_likes` tables. Cross-cutting: Shared Zod schemas and tRPC middleware with Integrations Router. (Verified with Context7 MCP.)
chunk.index_updates: chunk_1: id: chunk_1 type: chunk status: backlog linked_prd: prd-001 source_file: .ai/backlog/chunk_1.chunk.md
chunk.commit_instructions: git add .ai/backlog/chunk_1.chunk.md .ai/index.yml git commit -m "Chunk PRD for Prompts Router with cross-cutting analysis" git push origin main

chunk.summary: This chunk covers the Integrations tRPC router for `/integrations` page, enabling API key management with tRPC, Zod, and Drizzle-ORM. It targets `user_api_keys` table. Cross-cutting: Shared Zod schemas and tRPC middleware with Prompts Router. (Verified with Context7 MCP.)
chunk.index_updates: chunk_2: id: chunk_2 type: chunk status: backlog linked_prd: prd-001 source_file: .ai/backlog/chunk_2.chunk.md
chunk.commit_instructions: git add .ai/backlog/chunk_2.chunk.md .ai/index.yml git commit -m "Chunk PRD for Integrations Router with cross-cutting analysis" git push origin main
